operating_state: Total sales and revenues were $15.8 billion in Q1 2024, essentially flat versus $15.9 billion in Q1 2023, with lower sales volume offset by favorable price realization of $575 million and higher Financial Products revenues. Energy & Transportation increased on higher volume and pricing while Construction Industries and Resource Industries declined on lower equipment sales to end users. North America rose on favorable pricing while international markets declined on reduced volume.
cost_structure: Operating profit margin expanded to 22.3% from 17.2% in the prior year, with cost of goods sold improving to $9.7 billion from $10.1 billion despite lower revenue volumes. The improvement was driven by favorable price realization of $575 million and absence of the 2023 Longwall divestiture impact of $586 million, partially offset by lower sales volume profit impact. Operating expenses increased due to strategic investments in services growth, technology, autonomy, alternative fuels, and electrification initiatives.
material_events: The company completed divestiture of a non-US mining entity, generating $64 million in restructuring income. Share repurchases totaled $4.5 billion in Q1 2024 compared to $400 million in the prior year period, with $3.37 billion remaining under the 2022 Authorization. The Board maintained the quarterly dividend at $1.30 per share.
macro_exposures: The company continues monitoring supply chain disruptions, inflationary cost pressures, and labor constraints affecting transportation, components, and raw materials. Economic weakness in Europe is expected to continue while China excavator demand above 10-tons remains at relatively low levels. Currency impacts were unfavorable by $30 million, primarily related to Australian dollar weakness.
forward_outlook: Management expects full-year 2024 sales and revenues to be broadly similar to 2023 with slightly favorable price realization and no significant change in machine dealer inventories. Manufacturing costs are expected to remain flat while operating expenses will increase for strategic investments. Restructuring costs are projected between $300-450 million with capital expenditures of $2.0-2.5 billion.
uncertainty_profile: Order backlog of approximately $27.9 billion increased $0.4 billion from Q4 2023, with $6.0 billion not expected to be filled within twelve months. The company maintains covenant compliance under its credit facility with consolidated net worth of $17.70 billion above the $9.00 billion requirement.
